title: Incursa.Quic Public API Surface Verification
verification_method: Execution, inspection, and API-diff evidence from the public API analyzer, plus targeted surface tests derived from the Microsoft public-surface behavior baseline and the existing connection/runtime seams.
preconditions: The approved SPEC-QUIC-API and ARC-QUIC-API-0001 artifacts exist.
The Incursa.Quic project builds with public API analyzer support enabled.
The consumer facade types are available for inspection or promotion.
The Microsoft reference tests used as behavioral evidence have been reviewed and mapped to the refined surface expectations.
scope: This verification slice covers the connection-, listener-, and first client-entry consumer public API surface, the close/dispose/error projection visible through that surface, the server-side listener validation and accept semantics, the pending client-connect validation/cancellation/terminal semantics, and the internal-only boundary. It proves that only the approved facade types are public, that `QuicStreamType` has the direction-only shape, that `QuicListener` and `QuicListenerOptions` honor validation, cancellation, and disposal, that `QuicClientConnectionOptions` and `QuicConnection.ConnectAsync(...)` expose only the approved shape, and that the listed helper/runtime seam types remain internal. It does not attempt to prove a positive client connect path, full TLS-auth, wire behavior, handshake correctness, packet parsing, or performance.
expected_result: The public API matches the approved connection-, listener-, and first client-entry consumer surface, the close/dispose/error projection is visible, `QuicStreamType` has the approved direction-only shape, the listener validation and accept semantics are honest, pending client connect is honest about cancellation and unsupported TLS settings, and the helper/runtime seam types remain internal.
procedure: Compare src/Incursa.Quic/PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt and any future API approval diffs against the approved type list.
Run compile-time or reflection checks that the approved public types and members are present.
Run negative access checks that the helper and runtime seam types cannot be referenced from outside the assembly.

Add or review focused consumer-behavior tests for `QuicStreamType` shape, close/dispose/error mapping, read-side terminal behavior, listener validation, accept cancellation/disposal, narrow callback invocation, client option/connect validation, pending-connect cancellation/disposal, unsupported TLS rejection, and facade-to-runtime/host mapping.
Review the design note, spec, and the reference Microsoft test evidence together to confirm no extra public abstractions were introduced.
status_summary: Execution evidence for the earlier listener/server slice exists, and this verification plan now expands to the first client-entry shell. The proof target is client option/connect validation, unsupported TLS rejection, and pending terminal honesty; positive client establishment and full TLS-auth remain out of scope.

supplemental_sections.heading: Evidence Notes
supplemental_sections.content: The current proof includes build output and focused requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-API-0001`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0002`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0005`, and `REQ-QUIC-API-0008`. The next evidence addendum for this artifact is expected to cover the client-entry slice with option/connect validation, unsupported TLS rejection, and pending connect cancellation/disposal, while still leaving positive client establishment out of scope.

supplemental_sections.heading: Reference Behavioral Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicListenerTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicConnectionTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamConnectedStreamConformanceTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicCipherSuitesPolicyTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicTests.cs`

These tests are not target-repo proof, but they define the baseline public-behavior expectations that the target verification plan should mirror.
